title: Heapify the Array with Unique Elements

statement.markdown: Implement a method to heapify a given array. Note that heap must contain unique elements at all times, i.e., duplicates should be removed from the array before heapifying.
statement.inputFormat: First line contains the array size, second line contains the array elements.
statement.outputFormat: The heapified array elements (one element per line)
statement.notes: Preserve the relative order of elements if they are equal.

examples.explanation: The given array [3, 2, 1, 3, 2] contains duplicates. The unique elements are [1, 2, 3]. Heapifying this array results in [3, 2, 1] as -1 is used to replace removed element.

hints.text: Handle duplicates at the beginning by removing them before heapifying

editorial.title: Heapify with Unique Elements
editorial.intuition: Use a set to remove duplicates before heapifying.
editorial.bruteForce: Heapify the array with duplicates and then remove duplicates. However, this approach is inefficient (O(n log n)).
editorial.betterSolution: Remove duplicates from the array at the beginning, then heapify.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a set to store unique elements in O(n) time, then convert set to array and heapify.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The final heap satisfies the heap property and all elements are unique.
editorial.dryRun: Example run with array [1, 2, 1, 3, 2] and set {1, 2, 3}. Heapify [3, 2, 1].
